remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: NVIDIA OpenShell Secures the Agent. Who Governs the Fleet?
remarks.content: In short: At GTC 2026, NVIDIA released OpenShell, an open source runtime that sandboxes autonomous AI agents with kernel-level policy: what files they can touch, what processes they can spawn, where their traffic can go.... The post NVIDIA OpenShell Secures the Agent. Who Governs the Fleet? appeared
remarks.contentTranslation: 简而言之：在GTC 2026上，NVIDIA发布了OpenShell，这是一个开源运行时，通过内核级策略对自主AI代理进行沙盒隔离：它们可以访问哪些文件、可以生成哪些进程、流量可以流向何处……《NVIDIA OpenShell 守护代理安全，谁来管理整个集群？》一文已发布。
remarks.translation: NVIDIA OpenShell 守护智能体安全。谁来管理整个智能体集群？
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.text: A Fireside Chat with Cat and Thariq from the Claude Code team
remarks.content: Earlier this month I hosted a fireside chat session at the AI Engineer World's Fair with Cat Wu and Thariq Shihipar from Anthropic's Claude Code team. We talked about Claude Code, Claude Tag, Fable, coding agent security, evals, tool design, a
remarks.contentTranslation: 本月初，我在AI工程师世界博览会主持了一场炉边谈话，与Anthropic公司Claude Code团队的Cat Wu和Thariq Shihipar进行了交流。我们讨论了Claude Code、Claude Tag、Fable、编码代理安全、评估、工具设计等话题。
remarks.translation: 与Claude Code团队的Cat和Thariq围炉夜话

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
very notable trajectory comparison writeup here buried in the RLM paper from @a1zhang and @lateinteraction.

an open secret of "frontier" model training is that even without training on test, you can basically cheat by training on test lookalikes, enabling you to goalseek almost any benchmark number you want.

however when they are released open weights, 99% of the time the norm is that you do not get the datasets/rlenvs that would easily show you if someone was training on Temu Tbench, so there is plausible deniability. Alex and Omar discuss applying standard NLP distance metrics on hidden trajectories. There's no ultimate solution here, but they have some prelim explorations. It happens to support the finding that RLMs can generalize to unseen tasks that share latent structure observed in training.
remarks.translation: 这里有一份非常值得关注的轨迹对比分析，深藏在@a1zhang和@lateinteraction的RLM论文中。"前沿"模型训练的一个公开秘密是，即使没有在测试集上训练，你基本上也可以通过训练与测试集相似的数据来作弊，从而让你能够精准追逐几乎任何你想要的基准分数。然而，当这些模型以开放权重形式发布时，99%的情况下，你通常拿不到那些能轻易揭示是否有人在Temu Tbench上训练过的数据集或强化学习环境，因此就有了似是而非的否认空间。Alex和Omar探讨了在隐藏轨迹上应用标准NLP距离度量。这里没有终极解决方案，但他们做了一些初步探索。这恰好支持了这样一个发现：RLM能够泛化到那些与训练中观察到的潜在结构共享的未见任务上。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Led Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
The road to AGI is paved with economically valuable tasks.

That’s why enterprise AI is one of the most important frontiers. It’s where many of those tasks live.
remarks.translation: 通往通用人工智能的道路，是由具有经济价值的任务铺就的。正因如此，企业级人工智能是最重要的前沿领域之一——因为大量此类任务正存在于其中。

remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: Reverse-engineering is cheap now
remarks.content: I keep hearing anecdotes from people who used coding agents to reverse-engineer and automate devices in their homes. I think this is an interesting illustration of the impact of the reduced cost of writing code. Prior to agents, it was entirely possible to reverse-engineer home devi
remarks.contentTranslation: 我不断听到有人利用编程代理逆向工程并自动化家中设备的轶事。我认为这生动地说明了编写代码成本降低所带来的影响。在代理出现之前，对家用设备进行逆向工程是完全可行的，
remarks.translation: 逆向工程现在成本很低。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text: The big lesson from AI is that everything is code. A slide deck is code. Design is code. That cool promo video? Code. Excel automation? Code. The universe? Probably made of code too.
remarks.translation: AI带来的重要启示是：万物皆代码。幻灯片是代码，设计是代码。那个酷炫的宣传视频？也是代码。Excel自动化？还是代码。宇宙？大概也是代码构成的。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
Use one agent to do the work and another to review it against a rubric.

@trq212 explains how this works for video shorts:

“For something like, ‘Is this a good video short or not?’ you don’t have a deterministic answer.

You should have a separate verification agent read a rubric, review the short, and give feedback.

We call it self-preferential bias. When a model prefers its own output, it’s going to be more lenient at verifying it.”

📌 Watch the full episode here: https://t.co/mMrt4D5qU7
remarks.translation: 用一个智能体执行任务，再用另一个智能体根据评分标准进行审核。@trq212 解释了这种方法在短视频中的应用：“对于像‘这个短视频好不好’这类问题，并没有确定的答案。你应该让一个独立的验证智能体阅读评分标准、审核短视频并给出反馈。我们称之为自我偏好偏差。当模型偏爱自己的输出时，它在验证时会更加宽容。” 📌 观看完整剧集：https://t.co/mMrt4D5qU7
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
There are basically two kinds of companies now. The ones built before coding agents showed up, scrambling to retrofit. And the ones founded after. 

The second type is different from day one:
- Teams under ten people because they actually don't need more
- Work organized by projects, not departments
- Each person closes their own loop
- Almost no internal meetings
remarks.translation:
现在基本只有两类公司。一类是在编程智能体出现前成立的，正手忙脚乱地搞改造；另一类是在那之后创立的。第二类从第一天起就与众不同：  
- 团队不到十人，因为确实不需要更多人  
- 工作按项目组织，而非部门  
- 每个人独立闭环  
- 几乎没有内部会议
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

Top title for this cohort?

remarks.text: Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
remarks.content: In Rewriting Bun in Rust Jarred Sumner made the following claim: Claude Code v2.1.181 (released June 17th) and later use the Rust port of Bun. Startup got 10% faster on Linux but otherwise, barely anyone noticed. Boring is good.</
remarks.contentTranslation: 在《用Rust重写Bun》一文中，Jared Sumner提出了以下观点：Claude Code v2.1.181（于6月17日发布）及后续版本使用了Bun的Rust移植版。启动速度在Linux上提升了10%，但除此之外几乎无人察觉。无趣即是好事。
remarks.translation: Claude Code 现在使用基于 Rust 编写的 Bun。

remarks.text: Spot birds not golf
remarks.content: Suggestion for hyperscalers feeling pressure over data center water use: Buy up a few exclusive country clubs, convert the golf courses into public parks, pay for guides and binoculars to get the previous members into birdwatching - help them embrace a more sustainable hobby! Google

remarks.content: On file deletions. We’ve investigated a handful of reports where GPT-5.6 unexpectedly deleted files. What we have found is that this most commonly occurs when: Full access mode is enabled and codex
remarks.contentTranslation: 关于文件删除问题。我们已对若干关于GPT-5.6意外删除文件的报告进行了调查。调查发现，此类情况最常见于：启用完全访问模式且代码执行功能开启时。

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: 为什么前沿生态系统必须开放——Matei Zaharia 与 Reynold Xin，Databricks
podcasts.originalTitle: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
podcasts.excerpt: 我们非常高兴地欢迎 Databricks 加入 AIEWF，与数百家 AI 工程师生态系统的顶尖公司齐聚一堂。LS 订阅用户可使用专属折扣，在晚鸟票价格基础上再享优惠，并获得价值超过 $50k 的赞助商礼遇！大家仍在热议 Satya 的前沿生态系统文章，但真正动手构建的人却寥寥无几（<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…
podcasts.originalExcerpt: We’re excited to have Databricks join us at AIEWF, among hundreds of the top companies in the AI Engineer ecosystem. LS subscribers can use their discount to get past the late bird pricing and access over $50k in sponsor offers! Everyone is still talking about Satya’s Frontier Ecosystems post, but few have actually built a (<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: 打造一所让AI模型学习人性的学校
podcasts.originalTitle: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
podcasts.excerpt: 如果规模定律成立——Surge AI 首席执行官埃德温·陈相信它们确实成立——我们正飞速冲向一个未来，在那里，人类能做的任何事，AI 都能做得更好。当 OpenAI 的模型运用新颖的代数几何技术，推翻了数学家保罗·埃尔德什提出的一个公开猜想时，菲尔兹奖得主蒂莫西·高尔斯敏锐地感受到了这种转变。他起初以为模型证明了一个上界，并做好了心理准备：那将意味着“数学家很快就要完蛋了”。当他意识到它只是找到了一个反例时，他松了一口气——这为他争取了一两年时间，让他毕生投入的事业不至于那么快就被 AI 做得更好。作为为各大模型公司提供训练模型所用的数据环境和评估系统的公司创始人和首席执行官，陈对 AI 模型吸收那些我们曾认为是人类独有任务的速度，有着独特的视角……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: If scaling laws hold—and Surge AI CEO Edwin Chen believes they do—we’re hurtling toward a future where there’s nothing humans can do that AI can’t do better. When OpenAI’s models disproved an open conjecture posed by mathematician Paul Erdős using novel algebraic geometry techniques, Fields medalist Timothy Gowers felt the shift acutely. He initially thought the model had proved an upper bound, and braced himself: that would mean it was “all over for mathematicians very soon.” When he realized it had only found a counterexample, he was relieved—it bought him another year or two before the thing he’s devoted his life to becomes something AI does better.As founder and CEO of the company behind the data environments and evals the major model companies use to train their models, Chen has a unique perspective on how quickly AI models are absorbing tasks we used to think of as uniqu…
